rulings.Case Title: JACK BARLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. The motion is based on exposure to defendant's products. Defendant's separate statement does not raise the issue of replacement parts. It does not appear that defendant has shifted the burden of producing evidence given plaintiff's identification of Anton on the U.S.S. Parsons. Plaintiff also presents evidence through the declarations of Anton and Ay that reaise a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to October 28, 2008 per ex parte order. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD FULTON et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT J.T. THORPE & SONS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of producing evidence for defendant may not simply rely on discovery responses where plaintiff has filed objections or has not provided a verification and the testimony decedent's widow in inadequate. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD FULTON et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT ABBOTT STREET RADIATOR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied for failure to shift the burden of persuasion. Defendant's declarations fail to set forth the requisite foundational facts under Evidence Code sections 1272 and 403 to support their testimony that decedent did not work or do business with Abbott or explain how it is the declarants know that Abbott did not make or use asbestos products prior to their working for the company or how it is they learned what products the company made and the components thereof after they joined the company. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD FULTON et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT ABBOTT STREET RADIATOR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar. Duplicate entry. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES CAMERON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT NATIONAL BULK CARRIERS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to October 9, 2008 on the Court's own motion. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT DORTON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT NIBCO, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion is denied. It does not appear that defendant has shifted the burden by demonstrating that plaintiff's work did not expose him to asbestos. Further, the declarations of plaintiff and Ay create triable issue of fact. The motion for summary adjudication is denied. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO VS. D.N. SECURITY SERVICES, LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs;Application
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 7. PLAINTIFF MANUEL MUNOZ-ALFARO'S Application For Attorney'S Fees And Costs. The hearing is continued on the Court's own motion to October 3. Plaintiff is to file a declaration by October 1, that addresses the Schwachter Declaration of September 22, 2008, and to explain why plaintiff may recover fees beyond those attributable to plaintiff's claim and for preparing a motion in anticipation of a discovery response. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LEONORE DEATON VS. 16TH & DELORES ASSOCIATES L.P. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Per Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 9. PLAINTIFF LEONORE DEATON'S Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement Per CCP 664.6 is denied. Plaintiff has failed to present evidence of a settlement agreement that may be enforced under CCP 664.6. No evidence of a written agreement signed by the parties or agreed to by the parties on the record before the Court has been shown. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCIS DAVID RYAN VS. GUNTHER DERTZ
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANT GUNTHER DERTZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to October 24, 2008. Opposing party to provide courtesy copies of its papers to Department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK DUCKWALD et al VS. HANUS JELINEK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-08-626919 WITH CASE CGC-08-472638
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 11. PLAINTIFFS PATRICK DUCKWALD AND DENIZ DUCKWALD'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CUD-08-626919 WITH CASE CGC-08-472638. A hearing is required. It appears that the issues in the cases are related. At the same time, Jelinek is entitled to an expedited resolution of his claim. The parties are to appear and present to the Court an expedited discovery and summary judgment schedule that exceeds the time constraints for an unlawful detainer action but is less than the time limits prescribed for a civil action. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANTS EASTON SPORTS, INC AND EASTON-BELL SPORTS, INC'S Motion For Order Requiring Plaintiffs To Post An Undertaking To Secure The Award Of Costs is off calendar. Case transferred to Sonoma County. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET HATLEY VS. CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC., A CALIFORNIA et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn To Transfer Venue To Sacramento County
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 25, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANT CHARLES SCHWAB & CO., INC'S Motion To Transfer Venue To Sacramento County is granted based on Government Code section 12965 (b). The parties agree that section 12965 (b) is the controlling venue statute. The declarations submitted by the defendants demonstrate that Sacramento County is the proper venue for this dispute. The decision to terminate plaintiff occurred there, the overwhelming number of relevant records are there and this is the location where plaintiff would have worked but for defendants' alleged wrongful acts. =(302/PJM)
